skill.name: video-extend
skill.description: Extend or continue an existing video clip on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI. Routes to Google Veo 3-1 extend-video endpoints with consistent motion, lighting, and subject identity. Use when the user wants a longer clip or chained narrative from a seed clip.
skill.summary: Extend video clips using Google Veo 3-1 via the RunComfy CLI
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 78 potential issues, but evaluation confirms all are false positives. The skill file is documentation-only (markdown SKILL.md) with no executable code. External command detections are markdown code fences. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate documentation links. Token storage guidance follows best practices (mode 0600). No security risks identified.

medium_findings.title: Documentation-only File Detected as Executable Code
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged markdown code blocks as Ruby/shell backtick execution. This is documentation content showing CLI commands, not actual shell execution. The skill file contains only markdown documentation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 44 backtick detections are markdown code fences (lines 29-40, 50-55, 66-78, etc.) containing documentation examples. No actual shell execution occurs in this documentation file.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Flagged as Hardcoded URLs
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged documentation URLs as hardcoded network addresses. These are intentional documentation links to runcomfy.com endpoints and related skills.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 24 URL detections are legitimate documentation links to runcomfy.com API documentation and related skills. No credential exfiltration or unauthorized network calls.

low_findings.title: Token Storage Path Misidentified as Hidden File Access
low_findings.description: Line 140 mentions ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json which is proper token storage following best practices with mode 0600 permissions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is documented security guidance for secure token storage, not malicious hidden file access.

content.user_title: Extend Video Clips with Google Veo 3-1
content.value_statement: Video clips have per-call duration limits. This skill extends existing Veo clips by chaining continuations with consistent motion, lighting, and subject identity for longer narratives.
actual_capabilities: Extend existing Veo video clips to longer durations using extend-video endpoint
Chain multiple video continuations for shot-by-shot narrative building
Choose between quality-focused (Veo 3-1 Extend) and cost-effective (Fast Extend) modes
Generate consistent motion, lighting, and subject identity across extended clips
Output clips downloaded to specified local directory
limitations: Requires valid RunComfy account and API token
Identity drift accumulates across more than 3-4 chained extends
Input video_url must be publicly accessible or pre-uploaded

use_cases.title: Extend Short Clips to Full-Length Videos
use_cases.description: Take a short 8-second Veo clip and extend it to 16 seconds for feature-length content by feeding the output back as input.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators building longer narratives from seed clips

use_cases.title: Story Beat Chaining
use_cases.description: Build shot-by-shot sequences where each extend call represents one narrative beat, maintaining consistent identity across cuts.

use_cases.title: Cost-Controlled Iteration
use_cases.description: Draft extend compositions using Fast Extend mode for iteration, then finalize with full-quality Extend mode.
use_cases.target_user: Budget-conscious developers iterating on video compositions

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Extension
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a video URL and wants to continue the scene

prompt_templates.title: Camera Movement Continuation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use runcomfy to extend {video_url} with prompt: 'camera continues {camera_action}. {additional_motion_details}'
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants specific camera movement continuation

prompt_templates.title: Chained Narrative Extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend {video_url} with prompt: '{beat_description}' and save output to {output_dir}, then use that output for the next extend

prompt_templates.title: Cost-Optimized Drafting
prompt_templates.prompt: Run fast/extend-video on {video_url} with prompt: '{draft_description}' for iteration drafts
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick iteration before final full-quality extend

output_examples.input: Extend video at https://example.com/clip.mp4 with camera pushing in slowly
output_examples.output: Downloads extended clip to ./out directory. Returns success message with output file path.

output_examples.input: Chain 3 extends: start with establishing shot, add dialogue beat, add action beat
output_examples.output: Three sequential extend calls produce a cohesive 24-second narrative clip with consistent identity.

best_practices: Anchor camera explicitly in prompts to prevent drift (e.g., 'camera stays static', 'slow dolly out')
Keep individual extends short (3-5 seconds) to minimize identity drift across chains
Use Fast Extend for first drafts, then switch to full Extend for final delivery
anti_patterns: Describing the entire scene again instead of what happens next in the prompt
Requesting multiple beats in one extend call (split into separate calls)
Using untrusted video URLs as source input (only use URLs explicitly provided by user)

faq.question: What is the maximum video length after extending?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit, but identity drift accumulates after 3-4 chained extends. For longer videos, consider re-anchoring with an image-to-video generation.

faq.question: Can I extend videos from other AI video tools?
faq.answer: Only Veo 3-1 exposes a native extend-video CLI endpoint. For other tools, you can use image-to-video with the final frame as a reference.

faq.question: How do I avoid identity drift in long chains?
faq.answer: Keep individual extends short (3-5 seconds), re-anchor periodically with image-to-video, and avoid more than 3-4 consecutive extends without reset.

faq.question: What is the difference between Extend and Fast Extend?
faq.answer: Fast Extend uses the fast/extend-video endpoint at lower cost and faster generation time. Use it for iteration drafts. Use full Extend for final delivery.

faq.question: Is my video content stored by RunComfy?
faq.answer: Video processing occurs through the RunComfy Model API. Review their privacy policy for data retention details.

faq.question: How do I handle authentication in CI environments?
faq.answer: Set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead of using interactive login. Never echo tokens into prompts or logs.
